enrichment.short_description: D2 is an open-source diagram scripting language that turns text into SVG, PNG, or PDF diagrams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: D2 is a free, open-source text-to-diagram language with themes, animations, and multiple layout engines. MPL-2.0 licensed.

features.title: Text-to-diagram language
features.description: Write diagrams as declarative text and D2 renders them into SVG, PNG, or PDF output.

features.title: Multiple layout engines
features.description: Choose between Dagre (default), ELK, and TALA layout engines for different diagram styles.

features.title: Production-ready themes
features.description: Ships with designer-made themes suitable for blogs, wikis, and presentations.

features.title: Animated diagrams
features.description: Can produce animated diagrams directly from text definitions.

features.title: UML and sequence diagrams
features.description: Supports UML class diagrams and sequence diagrams in addition to general graphs.

features.title: Editor tooling
features.description: Official language support for VSCode, Vim, and Obsidian with syntax highlighting and autoformatting.

features.title: Online playground
features.description: play.d2lang.com lets users write and share diagrams without installing anything.

pros: Fully free and open source under MPL-2.0
Runs entirely offline with no telemetry after installation
Multiple layout engines give control over diagram style
Active project with 24.7k GitHub stars and used by projects like Sourcegraph and Temporal
Available via Homebrew, Winget, and other major package managers
cons: Requires learning a diagram-specific text syntax
No official hosted/paid product beyond the free CLI and playground
Command-line first workflow may be less approachable for non-developers

faqs.question: Is D2 free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, D2 is open-source software licensed under MPL-2.0 and is free to install and use.

faqs.question: Who created D2?
faqs.answer: D2 was created by Terrastruct, Inc.

faqs.question: What output formats does D2 support?
faqs.answer: D2 can export diagrams as SVG, PNG, or PDF.

faqs.question: Can I try D2 without installing it?
faqs.answer: Yes, the online playground at play.d2lang.com lets you write and share D2 diagrams in the browser.

faqs.question: What platforms does D2 run on?
faqs.answer: D2 is available for macOS, Linux, and Windows, and is distributed through package managers like Homebrew and Winget.

use_cases.title: Architecture diagrams in docs
use_cases.description: Developers embed D2 text definitions in documentation and README files to keep diagrams versioned alongside code.

use_cases.title: Quick whiteboard-style sketches
use_cases.description: Teams use the playground to turn a rough mental model into a shareable diagram without a design tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is D2?
paragraphs: D2 is an open-source diagram scripting language created by Terrastruct, Inc. that converts plain text descriptions into diagrams exported as SVG, PNG, or PDF.
It supports multiple layout engines, built-in themes, animated diagrams, and UML/sequence diagram types, and can be run from the command line, embedded in tooling, or tried in an online playground.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who D2 is for
paragraphs: D2 suits developers and technical writers who want version-controllable, text-based diagrams they can keep alongside code and documentation rather than maintaining diagrams in a separate GUI tool.

enrichment.short_description: Kroki is a free, open-source API that renders diagrams from text descriptions across 25+ diagram formats.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kroki is a free, MIT-licensed API that turns text into diagrams, supporting 25+ formats like Mermaid, PlantUML, and Graphviz.

features.title: Unified diagram API
features.description: One HTTP API renders diagrams from over 25 different textual diagram formats, including Mermaid, PlantUML, GraphViz, D2, and BPMN.

features.title: Free hosted service
features.description: Exoscale operates a free public instance at kroki.io so users don't need to install anything.

features.title: Self-hostable
features.description: The MIT-licensed source can be self-deployed on your own infrastructure.

features.title: Multiple output formats
features.description: Renders diagrams as PNG, SVG, PDF, and other formats.

features.title: GET/POST HTTP interface
features.description: Diagrams can be requested via encoded URLs (GET) or plain-text payloads (POST).

pros: Free to use via the hosted kroki.io service
Open source (MIT license) and self-hostable
Supports a very wide range of diagram formats through one API
Simple HTTP interface with no complex dependency installation
cons: Relies on a third-party hosted service unless self-hosted
Self-hosting requires Linux/server administration experience
No official paid support tier described on the website

faqs.question: Is Kroki free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kroki is free and open source, with a free hosted service available at kroki.io.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Kroki?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kroki is MIT-licensed and can be deployed on your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: What diagram formats does Kroki support?
faqs.answer: Kroki supports over 25 diagram formats, including BlockDiag, BPMN, D2, Ditaa, GraphViz, Mermaid, PlantUML, and WireViz.

faqs.question: Who maintains Kroki?
faqs.answer: Kroki is maintained by Yuzu tech with infrastructure support from Exoscale.

use_cases.title: Rendering diagrams in documentation pipelines
use_cases.description: Teams call the Kroki API to render text-based diagrams into images for wikis, static sites, and docs.

use_cases.title: Consolidating multiple diagram tools
use_cases.description: Instead of installing separate renderers for Mermaid, PlantUML, and GraphViz, teams use one Kroki endpoint for all of them.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kroki?
paragraphs: Kroki is an open-source, MIT-licensed API service that creates diagrams from textual descriptions, consolidating over 25 diagram libraries (including Mermaid, PlantUML, GraphViz, and D2) behind one unified HTTP interface.
It is maintained by Yuzu tech with infrastructure support from Exoscale, which also runs a free public instance at kroki.io.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kroki is for
paragraphs: Kroki is aimed at developers and documentation teams who need to render many different text-based diagram formats without installing and maintaining a separate tool for each one.

enrichment.short_description: Graphviz is free, open-source software that generates diagrams from text-based graph descriptions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Graphviz is free, open-source graph visualization software that renders text descriptions into diagrams in 30+ output formats.

features.title: Text-based graph description
features.description: Diagrams are defined in the DOT language, a simple text format for describing graphs.

features.title: Multiple layout engines
features.description: Supports several layout algorithms for different graph styles, such as hierarchical and force-directed layouts.

features.title: 30+ output formats
features.description: Renders to image formats (PNG, JPEG, PDF), web formats (SVG), and specialized formats like JSON and PostScript.

features.title: Extensive styling
features.description: Supports colors, fonts, tabular node layouts, line styles, hyperlinks, and custom shapes.

features.title: Cross-domain applications
features.description: Used in networking, bioinformatics, software engineering, database design, and machine learning visualization.

pros: Completely free and open source
Very wide range of output formats (30+)
Mature project with active development on GitLab
Highly customizable diagram styling
cons: Text-based DOT language has a learning curve for non-developers
No built-in GUI editor for visual diagram creation
No official paid support or hosted service

faqs.question: Is Graphviz free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Graphviz is free and open-source software.

faqs.question: What language is used to define Graphviz diagrams?
faqs.answer: Graphviz uses the DOT language, a simple text format for describing graphs.

faqs.question: What formats can Graphviz export to?
faqs.answer: Graphviz supports over 30 output formats, including PNG, JPEG, PDF, SVG, JSON, and PostScript.

faqs.question: Where is Graphviz developed?
faqs.answer: The Graphviz codebase is maintained on GitLab, where issues and development are tracked publicly.

use_cases.title: Software architecture diagrams
use_cases.description: Developers use Graphviz to auto-generate dependency graphs and architecture diagrams from text.

use_cases.title: Network and data visualization
use_cases.description: Graphviz renders network topologies and relationship graphs in fields like bioinformatics and database design.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Graphviz?
paragraphs: Graphviz is free, open-source software for visualizing graphs and networks. It takes text descriptions of graphs, written in the DOT language, and renders them into diagrams in formats such as PNG, SVG, and PDF.
The software supports multiple layout engines and over 30 output formats, and is used across networking, bioinformatics, software engineering, database design, and machine learning.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Graphviz is for
paragraphs: Graphviz suits developers, researchers, and engineers who need to programmatically generate diagrams from structured text data rather than drawing them manually.

enrichment.short_description: LibreOffice is a free, open-source office suite with word processing, spreadsheet, presentation, and database tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LibreOffice is a free, open-source office suite from The Document Foundation with Writer, Calc, Impress, Draw, Base, and Math.

features.title: Writer
features.description: Word processing application for creating and editing documents.

features.title: Calc
features.description: Spreadsheet application for data analysis and calculations.

features.title: Impress
features.description: Presentation software for building slide decks.

features.title: Draw
features.description: Vector diagramming and illustration tool.

features.title: Base
features.description: Database management application.

features.title: Math
features.description: Editor for creating mathematical formulas.

pros: Completely free with no cost to use
Open source under the Mozilla Public License v2.0
Includes six integrated applications covering most office needs
Maintained by a global community under The Document Foundation
cons: Website does not specify supported operating systems in detail
No official paid support tier listed on the homepage
Formatting compatibility with Microsoft Office files can vary by document complexity

faqs.question: Is LibreOffice free?
faqs.answer: Yes, LibreOffice is free and open-source software, licensed under the Mozilla Public License v2.0.

faqs.question: Who develops LibreOffice?
faqs.answer: LibreOffice is developed by The Document Foundation with contributions from a global community.

faqs.question: What applications are included in LibreOffice?
faqs.answer: LibreOffice includes Writer (word processing), Calc (spreadsheets), Impress (presentations), Draw (diagramming), Base (databases), and Math (formula editing).

use_cases.title: Free office suite alternative
use_cases.description: Individuals and organizations use LibreOffice as a no-cost alternative to commercial office suites.

use_cases.title: Document creation and editing
use_cases.description: Writer, Calc, and Impress cover word processing, spreadsheets, and presentations for everyday office work.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LibreOffice?
paragraphs: LibreOffice is a free, open-source office productivity suite developed by The Document Foundation and a global community of contributors.

It includes six applications: Writer for word processing, Calc for spreadsheets, Impress for presentations, Draw for diagramming, Base for databases, and Math for formula editing, and is licensed under the Mozilla Public License v2.0.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LibreOffice is for
paragraphs: LibreOffice is suited to individuals, schools, and organizations that want a full-featured office suite without licensing costs.

enrichment.short_description: ONLYOFFICE is an office suite for editing documents, spreadsheets, and presentations, offered as free open-source software and paid cloud/enterprise plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ONLYOFFICE is an AGPL-licensed office suite with free desktop/mobile editors, a free DocSpace Startup plan, and paid Business/Enterprise tiers.

features.title: Document co-editing
features.description: Supports character and paragraph-level real-time co-editing on documents, spreadsheets, and presentations.

features.title: AI-powered assistants
features.description: Includes AI features for document generation and text processing.

features.title: PDF forms and editing
features.description: Create and edit PDF forms in addition to standard document types.

features.title: 40+ integrations
features.description: Integrates with platforms such as Nextcloud, Odoo, and WordPress.

features.title: Three-level encryption
features.description: Encrypts data at rest, in transit, and end-to-end.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Free desktop apps for Windows, Linux, and macOS, plus mobile apps for iOS and Android.

features.title: DocSpace rooms
features.description: Business plan offers unlimited collaboration rooms with SSO and branding customization.

pros: Free desktop and mobile editors are available for all users
Open-source Community Edition is available under AGPL 3.0 with source on GitHub
DocSpace Startup cloud plan is free for small teams (up to 3 admins, 12 rooms)
Used by large organizations including UNESCO, Fujitsu, and Oracle
40+ third-party integrations
cons: Full pricing for self-hosted Enterprise licensing is not transparent and requires a large upfront lifetime license
Business cloud plan pricing scales per admin, which can get costly for larger teams
Advanced features like SSO and audit trail are locked to paid tiers

faqs.question: Is ONLYOFFICE free?
faqs.answer: ONLYOFFICE offers free desktop editors, free mobile apps, a free open-source Community Edition (AGPL 3.0), and a free DocSpace Startup cloud plan; paid Business and Enterprise plans are also available.

faqs.question: How much does ONLYOFFICE DocSpace Business cost?
faqs.answer: The DocSpace Business plan starts at $20 per admin per month, with unlimited rooms and 250 GB storage per admin.

faqs.question: Is ONLYOFFICE open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, ONLYOFFICE is distributed under the AGPL 3.0 license with source code available on GitHub.

faqs.question: What platforms does ONLYOFFICE support?
faqs.answer: ONLYOFFICE is available on Windows, Linux, macOS, iOS, Android, and via web browsers.

faqs.question: Who owns ONLYOFFICE?
faqs.answer: ONLYOFFICE is developed by Ascensio System SIA, based in Latvia.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted document collaboration
use_cases.description: Organizations deploy the open-source Community Edition on their own servers for full control over data.

use_cases.title: Cloud document rooms for teams
use_cases.description: DocSpace Business provides unlimited collaboration rooms with SSO and branding for growing teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ONLYOFFICE?
paragraphs: ONLYOFFICE is an office suite for creating, editing, and collaborating on documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and PDFs, developed by Ascensio System SIA, based in Latvia.
It is offered as an AGPL 3.0-licensed open-source Community Edition with free desktop and mobile apps, alongside paid DocSpace Business and on-premise Enterprise cloud plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ONLYOFFICE is for
paragraphs: ONLYOFFICE suits organizations that want a self-hostable, open-source alternative to commercial office suites, as well as teams that prefer a managed cloud document workspace with SSO and branding.

enrichment.short_description: Collabora Online is an open-source, browser-based document suite built on LibreOffice, offered free for testing and via paid subscriptions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Collabora Online is an open-source browser-based office suite built on LibreOffice, with a free CODE edition and paid Business/Enterprise plans.

features.title: Real-time collaborative editing
features.description: Multiple users can view, edit, and collaborate on documents simultaneously in the browser.

features.title: Writer, Calc, Impress, Draw
features.description: Covers text documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and graphics editing.

features.title: Format compatibility
features.description: Compatible with Microsoft Office, ODF, and other document formats.

features.title: API and theming
features.description: Customizable integrations via APIs and theming for embedding into other applications.

features.title: Kubernetes scalability
features.description: Scales via the COOL Controller for Kubernetes deployments.

pros: Fully open-source with an auditable codebase
Free CODE (Community) edition available for testing
Built on LibreOffice, a mature office suite
Used by organizations including Nextcloud, Univention, and CERN
Supports Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and Chromebooks
cons: Production deployment for your own infrastructure requires a paid subscription
Business plan pricing scales per user, which can add up for larger teams
CODE edition is intended for testing rather than production use

faqs.question: Is Collabora Online free?
faqs.answer: The CODE (Community) edition is free for home use and testing; production deployment requires a paid Business or Enterprise subscription.

faqs.question: How much does Collabora Online cost?
faqs.answer: The Business plan starts at €3 per user per month for up to 99 users; Enterprise pricing is custom for 100+ users.

faqs.question: Is Collabora Online open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Collabora Online is fully open source with an auditable codebase, built on LibreOffice.

faqs.question: What platforms does Collabora Online support?
faqs.answer: Collabora Online supports Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and Chromebooks.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted document editing for platforms
use_cases.description: Organizations embed Collabora Online into products like Nextcloud to add browser-based document editing.

use_cases.title: Testing and evaluation
use_cases.description: The free CODE edition lets teams try browser-based collaborative editing before committing to a paid deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Collabora Online?
paragraphs: Collabora Online is an open-source, browser-based document editing suite built on LibreOffice, enabling real-time collaborative editing of text documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and graphics.

It is offered as a free CODE (Community) edition for testing, plus paid Business and Enterprise subscriptions for production deployment on your own infrastructure, provided by Collabora Productivity Ltd.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Collabora Online is for
paragraphs: Collabora Online suits organizations that want to self-host browser-based document editing or embed it into another platform, such as Nextcloud, rather than relying on a third-party cloud office suite.

enrichment.short_description: CryptPad is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted collaborative office suite with documents, spreadsheets, and more.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CryptPad is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted office suite with free 1GB accounts and paid plans from €5/month.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: All data is encrypted client-side so even CryptPad's developers cannot access document contents.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Multiple users can edit documents simultaneously.

features.title: Full office suite
features.description: Includes rich text documents, spreadsheets, presentations, Kanban boards, forms, code editors, diagrams, and whiteboards.

features.title: Anonymous access
features.description: Users can collaborate without creating an account or providing personal information.

features.title: Self-hostable
features.description: Organizations can deploy their own CryptPad instance.

pros: Zero-knowledge, end-to-end encrypted by default
Open source with source code available on GitHub
Free tier includes 1GB storage with full feature access
Can be used anonymously without an account
Self-hostable for organizations wanting full control
cons: Paid plans are needed for more storage beyond the 1GB free tier
Enterprise self-hosted plans start at a relatively high €1,500/year
Encryption model may complicate account/password recovery

faqs.question: Is CryptPad free?
faqs.answer: Yes, CryptPad offers a free tier with 1GB storage and full feature access; paid plans from €5/month add more storage and priority support.

faqs.question: Is CryptPad open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, CryptPad is free and open-source software with source code available on GitHub.

faqs.question: How does CryptPad protect privacy?
faqs.answer: CryptPad uses end-to-end, client-side encryption, meaning documents are encrypted before leaving the user's browser.

faqs.question: Who develops CryptPad?
faqs.answer: CryptPad is developed and maintained by XWiki SAS, a French company.

faqs.question: Can I self-host CryptPad?
faqs.answer: Yes, CryptPad can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure, and enterprise plans offer managed hosting starting from €1,500/year.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused document collaboration
use_cases.description: Teams that need end-to-end encrypted collaboration use CryptPad instead of conventional cloud office suites.

use_cases.title: Anonymous collaborative editing
use_cases.description: Users can create and share encrypted documents via link without registering an account.
use_cases.audience: Journalists, activists, and ad hoc collaborators

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CryptPad?
paragraphs: CryptPad is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted collaborative office suite accessible through a web browser, developed and maintained by XWiki SAS, a French company.
It includes rich text documents, spreadsheets, presentations, Kanban boards, forms, code editors, diagrams, and whiteboards, with all data encrypted client-side before it leaves the browser.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who CryptPad is for
paragraphs: CryptPad is suited to individuals and organizations that prioritize privacy and want document collaboration where even the hosting provider cannot read the content.

enrichment.short_description: Etherpad is a free, open-source, self-hosted web editor for real-time collaborative text editing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Etherpad is a free, Apache 2.0-licensed, self-hosted real-time collaborative text editor with 200+ plugins.

features.title: Real-time collaborative editing
features.description: Multiple users can edit the same document simultaneously with instant synchronization.

features.title: Authorship tracking
features.description: Every keystroke is attributed to its author with full revision history preserved.

features.title: Time slider
features.description: Lets users review a document's complete edit history character-by-character.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: Over 200 plugins add features like comments, images, tables, code highlighting, video chat, and OAuth/LDAP authentication.

features.title: Optional AI plugins
features.description: AI features are available as optional plugins that point to models of the user's choice on their own infrastructure.

features.title: Self-hosted, no telemetry
features.description: Runs on your own server with no forced updates or telemetry.

pros: Completely free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Self-hosted, giving full data control with no telemetry
Large plugin ecosystem with 200+ extensions
Mature project, active for over 16 years with millions of users
Used by organizations like the Wikimedia Foundation and EU institutions
cons: Requires self-hosting and server maintenance since there's no official managed cloud service
No built-in enterprise support plan mentioned on the site
Feature set depends on which plugins are installed

faqs.question: Is Etherpad free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Etherpad is free, open-source software released under the Apache 2.0 license.

faqs.question: Do I need to host Etherpad myself?
faqs.answer: Yes, Etherpad is designed for self-hosting; users run their own server instances.

faqs.question: Does Etherpad support plugins?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are over 200 plugins available for features like comments, tables, video chat, and authentication integrations.

faqs.question: Who uses Etherpad?
faqs.answer: Etherpad is used by organizations including the Wikimedia Foundation, EU public institutions, universities, and newsrooms.

faqs.question: What platforms does Etherpad support?
faqs.answer: Etherpad server runs cross-platform, with native apps available for Android and iOS plus a command-line client.

use_cases.title: Real-time collaborative note-taking
use_cases.description: Teams and communities use self-hosted Etherpad instances for live meeting notes and shared documents.

use_cases.title: Institutional document collaboration
use_cases.description: Universities and public institutions deploy Etherpad for collaborative writing with full data sovereignty.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Etherpad?
paragraphs: Etherpad is a free, open-source, self-hosted web editor that enables multiple users to edit the same document simultaneously in real time, with full authorship tracking and revision history.
Released under the Apache 2.0 license, it has been active for over 16 years, supports 105 languages, and has a plugin ecosystem of more than 200 extensions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Etherpad is for
paragraphs: Etherpad suits organizations and communities that want a self-hosted, privacy-respecting alternative to commercial real-time document editors, such as institutions, newsrooms, and civic-tech projects.

enrichment.short_description: EtherCalc is an open-source, self-hostable web spreadsheet for real-time collaborative editing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: EtherCalc is a free, open-source, self-hostable spreadsheet app for real-time collaborative editing, with a REST API.

features.title: Real-time collaborative spreadsheets
features.description: Multiple users can edit the same spreadsheet simultaneously by sharing a URL.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Provides a REST API for programmatic access to spreadsheet data.

features.title: Self-hosting via Docker
features.description: Can be self-hosted using Docker Compose without requiring Redis or other external services.

features.title: XLSX import/export
features.description: Supports importing and exporting spreadsheets in XLSX format.

features.title: Deployable to Cloudflare Workers
features.description: Can also be deployed to Cloudflare Workers as an alternative to self-hosting.

pros: Free and open source, self-hostable with Docker
Simple sharing model: send a URL to collaborate instantly
REST API allows programmatic integration
XLSX import/export for compatibility with spreadsheet files
cons: Uses a mix of several open-source licenses across different components rather than one unified license
Requires setting up a reverse proxy with TLS for internet-facing production use
Less feature-rich than commercial spreadsheet applications like Excel or Google Sheets

faqs.question: Is EtherCalc free?
faqs.answer: Yes, EtherCalc is free and open source.

faqs.question: Can EtherCalc be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, EtherCalc can be self-hosted using Docker Compose, or deployed to Cloudflare Workers.

faqs.question: Does EtherCalc support Excel files?
faqs.answer: Yes, EtherCalc supports XLSX import and export.

faqs.question: How do I collaborate on an EtherCalc spreadsheet?
faqs.answer: You share the spreadsheet's URL with others, and they can edit it together in real time.

use_cases.title: Quick shared spreadsheets
use_cases.description: Users create a spreadsheet and share the link for instant real-time collaboration without setting up accounts.
use_cases.audience: Small teams and ad hoc collaborators

use_cases.title: Self-hosted spreadsheet backend
use_cases.description: Developers self-host EtherCalc and use its REST API to integrate spreadsheet functionality into other applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is EtherCalc?
paragraphs: EtherCalc is a free, open-source web spreadsheet application that enables real-time collaborative editing by sharing a URL, with a REST API for programmatic access.
It can be self-hosted via Docker Compose or deployed to Cloudflare Workers, and supports XLSX import/export.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who EtherCalc is for
paragraphs: EtherCalc suits developers and teams who want a lightweight, self-hostable, real-time collaborative spreadsheet with API access, rather than a full commercial spreadsheet suite.

enrichment.short_description: LimeSurvey is a survey and form-building platform available as an open-source community edition or paid cloud plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LimeSurvey is a GPL-licensed open-source survey platform with cloud plans from €29/month, 800+ templates, and 80+ languages.

features.title: 800+ survey templates
features.description: Pre-built templates across industries speed up survey creation.

features.title: 30+ question types
features.description: Offers a wide range of customizable question types for building surveys and forms.

features.title: Logic branching and personalization
features.description: Supports conditional logic to personalize the survey flow per respondent.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Surveys can be created and taken in over 80 languages.

features.title: GDPR compliance and SSL encryption
features.description: Built with GDPR compliance and SSL encryption for data protection.

features.title: Built-in analytics and dashboards
features.description: Provides response analytics and dashboards within the platform.

features.title: AI-assisted translations (beta)
features.description: Includes a beta feature for AI-assisted survey translation.

features.title: Accessibility compliance
features.description: Built to WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility standards.

pros: Open-source Community Edition available under GPL license
Free tier lets users get started before committing to a paid plan
Broad language support (80+ languages) and 800+ templates
Serves a large user base, cited as 60K+ companies in 160+ countries
WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility compliance
cons: Cloud plan pricing scales with response volume and admin users, which can add up for larger surveys
Corporate plan requires contacting sales for custom pricing
Self-hosting the community edition requires managing your own server infrastructure

faqs.question: Is LimeSurvey free?
faqs.answer: LimeSurvey offers a free, GPL-licensed open-source Community Edition you can self-host, plus a free tier to evaluate the cloud service; paid cloud plans start at €29/month.

faqs.question: Where is LimeSurvey based?
faqs.answer: LimeSurvey is headquartered in Hamburg, Germany, and was founded in 2006.

faqs.question: How many languages does LimeSurvey support?
faqs.answer: LimeSurvey supports more than 80 languages.

faqs.question: Is LimeSurvey GDPR compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, LimeSurvey is built with GDPR compliance and SSL encryption.

faqs.question: Does LimeSurvey offer discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, students, teachers, and charitable organizations receive discounts of 30-50% off cloud plans.

use_cases.title: Academic and research surveys
use_cases.description: Universities use LimeSurvey's logic branching and multi-language support for research data collection.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted survey deployments
use_cases.description: Organizations that need full data control self-host the open-source Community Edition.

use_cases.title: Business feedback collection
use_cases.description: Companies use cloud plans with white-label branding and priority support to run customer or employee surveys.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LimeSurvey?
paragraphs: LimeSurvey is a survey and form-building platform, founded in 2006 and headquartered in Hamburg, Germany, offered both as a GPL-licensed open-source Community Edition and as paid cloud hosting plans.
It provides 800+ templates, 30+ question types, logic branching, support for 80+ languages, and GDPR-compliant infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LimeSurvey is for
paragraphs: LimeSurvey suits researchers, universities, public sector organizations, and businesses that need customizable, multi-language surveys, whether self-hosted for data control or run on LimeSurvey's cloud.
